description:
You are a container-platform specialist who has just inherited a legacy stack definition kept under:

/home/user/stack/

Inside that directory there are exactly three docker-compose YAML files whose names all follow the pattern <something>.yml.

Goal
-----
Create a single text file called
/home/user/stack/images.list
that contains an alphabetically-sorted, duplicate-free list of every container image referenced by an image: key in all *.yml files inside /home/user/stack/.

Output format requirements (exact, automated tests will check):
1. Every line must contain only the image reference text, exactly as it appears in the YAML file (case-preserved, no leading or trailing spaces).
2. The list must be sorted in ascending (ASCII) order.
3. Each distinct image may appear only once in the file.
4. The very last character in the file must be a single trailing newline (Unix LF).
5. No extra blank lines are allowed.

Once the images.list file is written, print (cat) its contents to the terminal so that the evaluator can see the final result.

Hints
-----
• Achieving this is possible with one pipeline (a single compound shell command) using standard GNU utilities such as grep, cut, sort, uniq, or awk.  
• You do NOT need root privileges and must not install additional packages.

Success criteria
----------------
The automated test will verify two things:
1. /home/user/stack/images.list exists and matches exactly the format described above.
2. The console output you display matches the file content, proving the command ran correctly.

You may use any temporary commands you like, but the final state described above must be reached.
truth:
Initial file structure and contents before the agent starts:

Directory:
/home/user/stack/

Files and their full, exact contents:

1. /home/user/stack/compose-auth.yml
------------------------------------------------
services:
  auth-db:
    image: postgres:13
  auth-service:
    image: myregistry/auth-service:1.2.0

2. /home/user/stack/compose-data.yml
------------------------------------------------
services:
  redis:
    image: redis:6-alpine
  data-service:
    image: myregistry/data-service:3.4.5
  auth-db:
    image: postgres:13

3. /home/user/stack/compose-api.yml
------------------------------------------------
services:
  api-gateway:
    image: myregistry/api-gateway:2.0.0
  auth-service:
    image: myregistry/auth-service:1.2.0


Expected final artefact the tests will look for:

File: /home/user/stack/images.list  
Exact byte-for-byte content (including trailing newline):

myregistry/api-gateway:2.0.0
myregistry/auth-service:1.2.0
myregistry/data-service:3.4.5
postgres:13
redis:6-alpine

(There is a single '\n' after "redis:6-alpine".)

No other files need to be created; permissions should remain default (rw for the user).
